You're running an unsigned public beta of The Civic Desk. Windows SmartScreen may warn on install; that's expected. Report issues on GitHub.

Drafting Article

Lead: Library hosts Open Chess Night: Longmont Public Library is hosting an open chess evening with basic instruction and a selection of books about the game. Editor context: This event provides a community activity opportunity for residents interested in chess, offering both practice and learning resources. Suggested treatment: brief. Newsworthiness: 10/20 (immediacy 3, impact 2, conflict 1, novelty 4). Why now: A new chess event is scheduled at the library, providing a community activity. Treat this as recurring/background unless the source shows a new vote, deadline, dollar amount, filing, outage, meeting item, or public impact. Suggested next step: Verify the location and timing details before drafting the story. What would make this publishable: Confirmation of the location, time, and any special guest instructors. Compare against beat memory before drafting; write a story only if there is a new reportable fact.
